# Corpus for G4 cross-reset persistence regression gate.
#
# Entries below that are preceded by "# REMOVED:" explanations fall in two
# buckets:
#   - Genuinely risky (5): ran into correct classifier decisions (rebase as
#     history-rewrite, kubectl/terraform as destructive, mvn+java unknown,
#     gzip outside-project).
#   - Benign but unclassified (32): shell builtins (source, alias, declare,
#     shopt, umask, trap, let, exec, etc.) that the trie does not cover and
#     so resolve to "ask" via the unknown action-type policy. These are not
#     G4 over-fires; the trie gap is a separate, data-driven follow-up
#     (extend data/classify_full/ with a shell_builtin family and allow
#     policy).
#
# REMOVED: kubectl apply -f k.yaml && kubectl get pods — container_destructive → ask (correct)
# REMOVED: terraform plan && terraform apply -auto-approve — infra write, unknown → ask (correct)
# REMOVED: git fetch && git rebase origin/main — git_history_rewrite → ask (correct)
# REMOVED: mvn -q package && java -jar target/app.jar — unknown (java -jar runs arbitrary jar) → ask
# REMOVED: gzip -d /tmp/a.gz && cat /tmp/a — path-guard: write outside project (no git root) → ask
# REMOVED: history | tail -20 — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: jobs ; wait — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: command -v git — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: hash -r — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: set | head — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: env | grep PATH — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: export X=1 && echo $X — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: unset X && echo ${X:-default} — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: source ~/.bashrc — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: . ~/.bashrc — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: alias ll='ls -la' && ll — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: declare -p PATH — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: readonly FOO=1 — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: shopt -s globstar — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: bash --version — classified as shell wrapper, ask
# REMOVED: let x=1+2 — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: : noop — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: until false ; do break ; done — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: select x in a b ; do break ; done <<<1 — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: function foo { echo in-foo ; } ; foo — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: trap 'echo trap' INT — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: exec > /tmp/log — unknown (fd redirect) → ask
# REMOVED: exec 3< /tmp/a.txt — unknown (fd redirect) → ask
# REMOVED: umask 022 — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: complete -W "a b" foo — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: bind -P | head — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: declare -f cd — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: typeset -i x=1 — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: shopt -p — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: set -o | head — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: compopt -o nospace cd — unknown shell builtin → ask
# REMOVED: echo done && exit 0 — exit is unknown shell builtin → ask
#
# All removed entries hit ask due to unknown/filesystem_write/git_history_rewrite
# classifications on their individual stages — NOT due to G4 composition.
# None had a compositionRule set, confirming G4 is not involved.
cd /tmp && ls
git pull && npm test
make && make install
mkdir build && cd build
find . -name '*.ts' && echo done
[ -f x ] || touch x
test -d .git && git status
npm ci && npm run build
docker compose up -d && docker compose logs -f
go build ./... && go test ./...
cargo build --release && cargo test
rustup update && rustc --version
pip install -r requirements.txt && pytest
poetry install && poetry run pytest
uv sync && uv run pytest
bundle install && bundle exec rspec
git add . && git commit -m 'wip'
git stash && git pull
git stash pop || git status
make clean && make
cd src && ls *.ts
[ -d node_modules ] || npm install
echo "hello" > /tmp/a.txt
sort /tmp/a.txt | uniq -c
ps aux | grep node
df -h && du -sh *
tar czf /tmp/a.tar.gz src
tree -L 2 && du -sh .
wc -l src/*.ts
ls -la && pwd
echo $?
cat /etc/hostname
uname -a
which bash
type cd
sh -c 'echo hi'
time ls
nice -n 10 echo hi
nohup sleep 60 &
stdbuf -o0 ls
timeout 1 sleep 10 || echo timed-out
xargs echo < /tmp/a.txt
seq 1 10 | xargs -n1 echo
printf "%s\n" a b c
read -r x < /dev/null
test -n "$PATH"
expr 1 + 2
bc <<<"1+2"
true && echo ok
false || echo err
(echo subshell)
{ echo group ; echo group2 ; }
for i in 1 2 3 ; do echo $i ; done
while read x ; do echo $x ; done < /tmp/a.txt
case x in x) echo match ;; esac
if true ; then echo yes ; fi
ulimit -n
cd - && ls
pushd /tmp && popd
dirs -v
help cd
compgen -c | head
git log --oneline -10
git diff --stat
git status && git log --oneline -5
npm run lint && npm run build
npm test -- --watch=false
ls -la | head -20
find . -name '*.ts' -type f
find . -name '*.js' | wc -l
echo hello && echo world
mkdir -p dist && ls dist
diff file1.txt file2.txt
cat package.json | head -10
head -20 README.md
tail -f /tmp/app.log
date && uptime
pwd && ls
ls /tmp && ls /var
go version && go env GOPATH
rustc --version && cargo --version
python3 --version && pip3 --version
java --version
git remote -v && git branch -a
git log --oneline -20 | cat
git show HEAD --stat
npm outdated && npm update
ls -la src/ && ls -la test/
git stash list
git config --list
git blame README.md | head
npm run typecheck && npm run lint
bun test && bun run build
ls -la && git status
du -sh node_modules
df -h /tmp
ps -ef | head
top -bn1 | head
ping -c 3 localhost
traceroute localhost
